title: Quantitative Reasoning, Estimation, and Problem Framing · Decision Notebook: Assumption Record
summary: Estimate attendance, supplies, time, and uncertainty for a neighborhood event before exact values are available. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg assumption record increment in a transparent event-planning estimate notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.
Check signs, scale, units, and limiting cases to reject unreasonable numerical answers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Assumption Record evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 20 units, changes by −7 units, then by +4 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Defend the Assumption Record model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Assumption Record applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
